channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Alberta man charged with excessive speeding on B.C. highway
item.description: A man from Alberta was arrested by police for allegedly driving at speeds exceeding three times the posted limit on a British Columbia highway. The incident, reported by CTV News, highlights concerns ...

item.title: Magnolia Secures Victory Against Meralco in PBA Match
item.description: The Philippine Basketball Association (PBA) saw a thrilling match on July 24, 2026, where the Magnolia Hotshots emerged victorious against the Meralco Bolts. The game, part of the Governors' Cup tourn...

item.title: Heavy rainfall and thunderstorms expected in Karachi and across Pakistan as PMD issues warnings
item.description: The Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) has issued forecasts of widespread rain, thunderstorms, and windstorms across southern and central Pakistan from July 25 to 27, with Karachi expected to ex...

item.title: Acer Chromebook Spin 514 on Sale with Additional Tech Deals
item.description: The article highlights a significant discount on Acer's Chromebook Plus Spin 514, originally priced at $749, now available for $559 at Best Buy. This 2-in-1 device features 12GB RAM, a MediaTek Kompan...

item.title: Voters Overestimate Gas Price Increases, Impacting Midterm Elections
item.description: A POLITICO analysis reveals that three in five Americans overestimate the rise in gas prices in their area, with significant implications for the upcoming midterms. The survey, conducted by Public Fir...

item.title: Tulia Recognizes Burkina Faso's Anti-Terror Efforts
item.description: The article discusses international recognition of Burkina Faso's efforts in combating terrorism. While the original text appears to be a placeholder or incomplete, the core message revolves around ac...

item.title: Three Terrorists Get Life Jail For Abducting Students In Oyo State
item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja has sentenced three men to life imprisonment for their roles in abducting schoolchildren and teachers in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State. The defendants, Abdu...

item.title: Richmond Hill Vehicle Fire Under Investigation for Arson
item.description: A fire that damaged two vehicles in Richmond Hill is being investigated by York Regional Police as a potential arson incident. Fire crews were called to a home on Gatcombe Circle in the Regent Street ...

item.title: Rural Entrepreneurs Access Loans to Develop Fuel Stations
item.description: A recent initiative by the Tanzanian government has enabled rural entrepreneurs to access financial support for establishing fuel stations across the country. This development aims to boost economic g...

item.title: MEA condemns China's illegal projects in Jammu and Kashmir
item.description: The Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) has strongly condemned China's ongoing illegal projects in Jammu and Kashmir, accusing the country of violating territorial integrity. The MEA statement highligh...

item.title: Youth Skepticism Toward AI Grows Amid Concerns Over Misinformation and Deepfakes
item.description: A growing number of young people are expressing skepticism toward artificial intelligence, despite its widespread adoption. According to market research firm YPulse, 37% of teens aged 13-17 cringe at ...

item.title: Bennelong in London: exploring identity, memory and historical representation through theatre
item.description: Bennelong in London, written by award-winning Muruwari playwright Jane Harrison, is a thought-provoking theatrical work that examines the complexities of Aboriginal history and cultural representation...

item.title: Pakistan Mediates Iran-US Talks Amid China's Initiative
item.description: Pakistan is actively exploring a diplomatic path to restart stalled US-Iran negotiations over the ongoing war, with efforts spearheaded by China. The initiative follows a recent visit by Iran's Interi...

item.title: Lena Waithe's 'The Chi' Celebrates Black Life and Longevity in Chicago
item.description: Lena Waithe's 'The Chi,' a groundbreaking television series that premiered in 2018, has concluded its historic eight-season run, marking it as the longest-running Black drama in premium cable history....

item.title: Beatrice Elachi's Kajiado Rural Home and Francis Atwoli's Influence
item.description: Former Nairobi Speaker Beatrice Elachi gave a rare tour of her Kajiado country home during a TV show, revealing she owned the property since 2010. She credited COTU boss Francis Atwoli for inspiring h...

item.title: ANC Expels Councillor Over Alleged DA Spying Allegations
item.description: The African National Congress (ANC) in the eThekwini Region has expelled Ward 8 councillor Marco Mbambo, accusing him of spying for the Democratic Alliance (DA) and planning to defect to the oppositio...

item.title: Lovi Poe Shares How Motherhood Has Made Her More Intentional
item.description: Lovi Poe, known for her roles in Philippine entertainment, recently opened up about how becoming a mother has transformed her approach to life and career. In an exclusive interview, she emphasized tha...

item.title: FG strengthens anti-bullying measures to ensure safe learning environments
item.description: The federal government has ramped up its efforts to combat bullying in schools, encouraging students to report incidents without fear. This renewed campaign is part of the Safe School Initiative, aime...

item.title: Sita Sargeant's Award Recognition Sparks Growth for Her Historical Women's Tours Business
item.description: Sita Sargeant, founder of She Shapes History, saw her business and cause take off after being named ACT's Young Australian of the Year. The award catalyzed explosive growth, with attendance on her wal...

item.title: Tens of thousands flee wildfires in France as Spain declares national emergency
item.description: Tens of thousands of people have been forced to flee their homes in southwest France due to devastating wildfires, while Spain has declared a national emergency as blazes merge near Madrid. The fires,...
